model_narrative: We run a distribution network of canning plants that ship product to a set of markets. The decision is how many cases to ship along each plant-to-market route, and we also track the total transportation cost that results. Shipping a case along a route costs an amount that grows with the distance and the freight rate. The objective is to make the total transportation cost as small as possible.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the supply locations, the canning plants that ship product out

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the demand locations, the markets that receive product

params.name: a
params.doc: the production capacity available at each plant, in cases

params.name: b
params.doc: the demand that must be met at each market, in cases

params.name: d
params.doc: the distance from a plant to a market, in thousands of miles

params.name: f
params.doc: the freight rate in dollars per case per thousand miles

params.name: c
params.doc: the transport cost per case shipped from a plant to a market, in thousands of dollars per case; derived from the freight rate and distance

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the quantity shipped from a plant to a market, in cases

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the total transportation cost over all shipments, in thousands of dollars

description: Set the total transportation cost equal to the cost of all shipments, where each route's shipment is valued at its per-case transport cost and these are added up over every plant-to-market route.

vars.doc: the quantity shipped from a plant to a market, in cases

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the total transportation cost over all shipments, in thousands of dollars

description: For each plant, the total quantity shipped out of that plant to all markets must not exceed the production capacity available at that plant.

model_narrative: We run a distribution network of canning plants that ship product to a set of markets. The decision is how many cases to ship along each plant-to-market route, and we also track the total transportation cost that results. Shipping a case along a route costs an amount that grows with the distance and the freight rate. The objective is to make the total transportation cost as small as possible.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the supply locations, the canning plants that ship product out

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the demand locations, the markets that receive product

params.name: a
params.doc: the production capacity available at each plant, in cases

params.doc: the distance from a plant to a market, in thousands of miles

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the quantity shipped from a plant to a market, in cases

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the total transportation cost over all shipments, in thousands of dollars

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, set the total transportation cost equal to the cost of all shipments, where each route's shipment is valued at its per-case transport cost and these are added up over every plant-to-market route. Second, for each plant, the total quantity shipped out of that plant to all markets must not exceed the production capacity available at that plant. Finally, for each market, the total quantity shipped into that market from all plants must be at least the demand at that market.
